name: Marjal dels Moros Loop Trail
name_original: La Marjal dels Moros — Circular desde Playa de Puzol
description: Explore the easy 4.8-mile (7.8 km) La Marjal dels Moros circular hike from Playa de Puzol, a protected wetland perfect for birdwatching.
region_intro: Hiking around Puçol offers diverse landscapes, from protected wetlands to coastal promenades and hilly terrain. The region is characterized by the flat, natural reserve of Marjal dels Moros, the Mediterranean coastline, and the foothills of the Sierra Calderona, including La Costera Municipal Natural Park. This varied topography provides a range of hiking experiences, from easy, flat walks to more challenging routes in the surrounding hills. Best hiking trails around Puçol The most popular hiking route is Marjal dels Moros Loop Trail, an easy 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 57 minutes to complete, offering opportunities for birdwatching in a protected wetland. Another top favourite among local hikers is Playa Puig de Santa Maria – El Puig Beach Promenade loop from Platja de Puçol, a moderate 9.0 miles (14.5 km) path showcasing coastal scenery and beach views. Local hikers also love the Puçol Beach Promenade loop from C/Riu Túria, 38, a 3.4 miles (5.4 km) trail leading through a coastal promenade, often completed in about 1 hour 22 minutes. Hiking around Puçol is defined by its wetlands, coastal paths, and hilly terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely strolls to more demanding ascents. The routes in Puçol are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 1,300 hikers have used komoot to explore Puçol's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Puçol?
A: Puçol offers a diverse network of over 40 hiking routes. These trails cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging ascents in the surrounding hills.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Puçol?
A: The region boasts a varied topography, including the protected wetlands of Marjal dels Moros, pleasant coastal promenades along the Mediterranean Sea, and the hilly terrain that forms the foothills of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners in Puçol?
A: Yes, Puçol has 11 easy routes perfect for families and beginners. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e819107946/marjal-dels-moros-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marjal dels Moros Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 7.8 km path with minimal elevation gain, ideal for birdwatching.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Puçol?
A: Many routes in Puçol are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38226569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puçol Beach Promenade loop from C/Riu Túria, 38&lt;/a&gt; offers a pleasant 5.4 km coastal walk.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I spot while hiking in Puçol?
A: The Marjal dels Moros protected wetland is a prime location for wildlife spotting, especially birdwatching. It's a natural reserve rich in diverse flora and fauna, featuring observation points specifically for this purpose.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions along the hiking trails in Puçol?
A: Yes, Puçol is located along the Camino del Cid, a cultural-historical long-distance hiking route. Additionally, you can explore nearby castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Serra&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3954829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sagunto Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which are accessible from various routes in the region.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Puçol?
A: Puçol offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide milder temperatures, making them ideal for longer walks. Even in winter, coastal paths can be enjoyable, while summer might be better suited for early morning or late afternoon hikes to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any moderate hiking options for those looking for a bit more challenge?
A: Absolutely. Puçol features 15 moderate routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31523811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sunset View – Church of Grau Vell loop from Platja de Puçol&lt;/a&gt;, a 10.2 km trail that offers scenic views and takes around 2.5 hours to complete.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic views in Puçol?
A: Yes, the foothills of the Sierra Calderona, including La Costera Municipal Natural Park, offer signposted trails with breathtaking views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garbí Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2606235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cross of Picayo Summit&lt;/a&gt; are notable highlights for panoramic vistas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Puçol?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 140 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the tranquil wetlands to the refreshing coastal paths, and the variety of routes suitable for all abilities.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking trails that pass through or near Puçol?
A: Yes, Puçol is part of the 'Conquest of Valencia' section of the Camino del Cid. Other long-distance trails like the TransIberianTrail and TransCalderona also intersect or are in close proximity to the region, offering opportunities for multi-day adventures.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for a longer day hike?
A: For those seeking a longer day out, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38224919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Puig de Santa Maria – El Puig Beach Promenade loop from Platja de Puçol&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.5 km path showcasing coastal scenery and beach views, typically taking around 3.5-4 hours.
tips: The Marjal dels Moros is a wetland that is part of a protected natural reserve of flora and fauna. A privileged site for ornithologists and bird watching lovers, along the route there will be several viewpoints for this purpose.
Environmental education center located within a native flora reserve. A very interesting site especially for those who are interested in the environment and its protection.
Bird watching point within the route through the paths of the Marjal. It is also a beautiful place from where you can see the Mediterranean coast and the Serra de la Calderona behind.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: This easy circular hike, covering approximately 7.76 km, can generally be completed in under two hours, making it a perfect option for a leisurely outing.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is considered easy and is ideal for individuals of all fitness and skill levels, including beginners and families. Its minimal elevation gain (around 10 meters) and accessible paths make it very family-friendly.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route conveniently starts near a parking lot, making access straightforward. You'll find parking available close to the beginning of the circular path from Playa de Puzol.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The path offers varied terrain, including paved sections (around 3.10 km), unpaved areas (approximately 1.32 km), and natural surfaces. You'll also encounter wooden boardwalks, especially in the birdwatching areas, which contribute to easy navigation.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see in Marjal dels Moros?
faqs.answer: The Marjal dels Moros is a protected natural reserve renowned for its rich biodiversity. It's a crucial habitat for approximately 250 bird species, including endangered ones like the marbled teal, white-headed duck, and ferruginous duck. Beyond birds, you might also spot endemic plant life, samaruc fish, and European pond turtles.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike here?
faqs.answer: While enjoyable year-round, the best time to visit for birdwatching is typically during migration seasons (spring and autumn) when the wetland is bustling with diverse bird species. Cooler months also offer more comfortable hiking conditions.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll experience the Marjal dels Moros Nature Reserve itself, a protected wetland. Key highlights include the Marjal dels Moros Wooden Boardwalk and Birdwatching Area, perfect for observing local fauna, and the Marjal dels Moros East Viewpoint, offering scenic vistas of the marsh.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths within the Marjal dels Moros area. You'll find sections that follow the Camí de la Mar, Camí de les 30 figueres, Camí de Rampete, Camí de Pedralta, and Pas de les Egües.

faqs.question: Is the La Marjal dels Moros — Circular from Playa de Puzol trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The Marjal dels Moros is a protected natural area. While specific dog policies can vary, generally, dogs are permitted on trails but must be kept on a leash to protect the sensitive wildlife and natural habitats. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike in Marjal dels Moros?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit, nor is there an entrance fee to access the trails within the Marjal dels Moros Nature Reserve. It is freely accessible to the public, allowing everyone to enjoy its natural beauty.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or cafes nearby?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is primarily natural, being close to Playa de Puzol means you'll find amenities like cafes, restaurants, and potentially restrooms in the nearby town or along the beach area before or after your hike.

waypoints.name: Marjal dels Moros Wooden Boardwalk and Birdwatching Area
